since_last: 58% of 89 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +0.53%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: Markets closed — Independence Day (observed)
cards.body: US equity markets are shut for Independence Day (observed), so the tape is frozen at the previous session's close. Everything below reflects that last close — not live trading — and refreshes when the market reopens.

cards.headline: Last close: a broad bid, 58% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX +0.00% · BTC +0.82% · Gold +1.70% · Nasdaq100 -1.61%. At the last close, 58% of 89 AI names were green — a broad bid. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware did the heavy lifting at +0.53%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Applications & Data Software lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +0.53%; weakest is AI Applications & Data Software at -0.98%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: PUBM is the standout at -8.93%
cards.body: Leaders: SYNA +2.07%, TER +1.67%, AMBA +1.19%, KLAC +1.04%, SMR +0.92%. Laggards: PUBM -8.93%, SAP -1.37%, SNOW -0.84%, IBM -0.66%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -0.62% at $160.99
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -0.62% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
